channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: First migrants sent to Albania for processing are returned to Italy
item.description: SHENGJIN, Albania (AP) — An Italian navy ship on Saturday took back to Italy the first 12 migrants from newly opened asylum processing centers in Albania following a court decision in Rome.
item.dc:creator: VLASOV SULAJ and LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Drone targets Israeli prime minister's house as strikes in Gaza kill 50
item.description: The Israeli government said a drone targeted the prime minister’s house Saturday, though there were no casualties, as Iran’s supreme leader vowed Hamas would continue its fight following the killing of the mastermind of last year’s deadly Oct. 7 attack.
item.dc:creator: Adam Schreck And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Belgrade's landmark Hotel Yugoslavia faces likely demolition, with many opposed
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Belgrade’s iconic Hotel Yugoslavia, once a symbol of progress in the former socialist state of Yugoslavia that broke apart in the 1990s and a favorite gathering place for local residents as well as world leaders, now stands in eerie silence awaiting its likely demolition.
item.dc:creator: DUSAN STOJANOVIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Freedom of expression threatened more seriously in Gaza than in any recent conflict, UN expert says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Freedom of expression has been threatened more seriously in Gaza than in any recent conflict, with journalists targeted in the war-torn territory and Palestinian supporters targeted in many countries, a United Nations expert said Friday. Irene Khan, the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Hezbollah says its war with Israel is entering a new phase
item.description: Lebanon’s Hezbollah militant group said Friday it is entering a new phase in its fight against invading Israeli troops, as the region reckons with the killing of top Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar in a battle with Israeli forces in Gaza. Hamas acknowledged Sinwar’s death and described him as a martyr.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea claims it discovered the remains of a South Korean drone in its capital
item.description: North Korea says it found the remains of a South Korean drone during a search in its capital, Pyongyang.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cuba’s grid goes offline with massive blackout after a major power plant fails
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Cuba’s electrical grid went down Friday after one of the island’s major power plants failed, a day after a massive blackout swept across the Caribbean island and with no official estimate for when service will be restored.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Cuban power plant failure leaves millions in the dark in island-wide outage
item.description: Millions of Cubans were left without power on Friday after a failure at a power plant, the communist government said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sinwar's ruthless brother Mohammed slated to take over as leader of Hamas
item.description: Mohammed Sinwar is expected to take over as leader of Hamas after his older brother Yahya Sinwar was confirmed to have been killed in Gaza by the Israel Defense Forces as the brutal war continues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea sending 10K troops to fight with Russia, Zelensky says
item.description: North Korea is sending around 10,000 troops to Ukraine to fight alongside Russian forces, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: A blaze burns down a brand-new fire station in Germany that lacked fire alarms
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A brand-new fire station in Germany, which was destroyed in a fire, causing millions of euros in damage and destroyed equipment did not have a fire alarm system, local media reported Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Efforts to combat deforestation in Colombia hindered because of armed groups, report finds
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Armed groups operating in Colombia's Amazon are tightening their grip on the region and that's stalling government efforts to tackle deforestation, according to a think tank report Thursday.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden says it's time to 'move on' in call with Netanyahu after Sinwar killing
item.description: President Biden congratulated Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Israel’s killing of Hamas chief Yahya Sinwar, the architect of the Oct. 7, 2023, attack that triggered a yearlong war and introduced new tensions between the leaders.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Former Mexican public security chief gets more than 38 years, $2M fine for taking cartel bribes
item.description: Mexico’s ex-public security secretary Genaro García Luna was sentenced to more than 38 years in prison and a $2 million fine for taking millions in bribes to protect the Sinaloa cartel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas terror leader Yahya Sinwar killed in Gaza, Israel says
item.description: Israeli officials announced Thursday that Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar was killed during military operations inside the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Christmas Island National Park travel guide where millions of colorful crabs migrate annually
item.description: From diving to birdwatching, there is a plethora of experiences to take part in at Christmas Island National Park. The red crab migration is one popular experience for tourists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden to travel to Germany Thursday, delays African leg of trip
item.description: In Berlin, Biden will convene a meeting with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz to discuss continued support for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Set sail on world’s largest cruise ship, which can accommodate over 7,000 guests per voyage
item.description: Icon of the Seas is the world's largest cruise ship. With extensive dining options, record-breaking pools and breathtaking live shows, there's something for everyone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah vows to expand attacks in Israel after deadly strike in Lebanon's Christian heartland
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — The day after a deadly Israeli airstrike in northern Lebanon – far from Hezbollah’s main area of influence – the militant group's acting leader said it would aim rockets into more areas of Israel.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China deploys record 125 warplanes in large-scale military drill in warning to Taiwan
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China employed a record 125 aircraft, as well as its Liaoning aircraft carrier and ships, in large-scale military exercises surrounding Taiwan and its outlying islands Monday, simulating the sealing off of key ports in a move that underscores the tense situation in the Taiwan S
item.dc:creator: JOHNSON LAI and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strike in northern Lebanon kills at least 21 people
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit an apartment building in northern Lebanon on Monday, killing at least 21 people, according to the Lebanese Red Cross. The Israeli military did not immediately comment and the target was not clear.
item.dc:creator: YAHYA HABCHITI, KAREEM CHEHAYEB, and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UN Security Council voices 'strong concern' for UN peacekeepers after Israeli attacks
item.description: It's the first statement by the powerful U.N. body since Israel's attacks on the positions of the peacekeeping force.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Vial linked to UK woman's death had enough poison to kill thousands
item.description: Sturgess and her partner collapsed after they came into contact with a discarded perfume bottle containing the nerve agent.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea is preparing to destroy northern parts of inter-Korean roads, Seoul says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea said Monday it has detected signs that North Korea is preparing to destroy the northern parts of inter-Korean roads no longer in use, as the rivals are embroiled in soaring tensions over North Korea’s claim that South Korea flew drones over its territory.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel says 4 soldiers killed by Hezbollah drone attack while Israeli strike in Gaza leaves 20 dead
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hezbollah drone attack on an army base in central Israel killed four soldiers and severely wounded seven others Sunday, the military said, in the deadliest strike by the militant group since Israel launched its ground invasion of Lebanon nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine cracks down on alleged draft dodgers
item.description: Ukrainian military recruitment officers raided restaurants, bars and a concert hall in Kyiv, checking military registration documents and detaining men who were not in compliance.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and ELISE MORTON, Associated Press, NewsNation
